sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — a thing that can only ever exist once, enforced by the language itself.
sketches.resonance: Some things in life can never be repeated or duplicated; the language guarantees there will never be another.

sketches.mechanism: A LEFT JOIN preserves every row from the left table even when no match exists on the right, filling unmatched columns with NULL.
sketches.resonance: We carry the shape of what was supposed to be beside us, even when nothing is there — the NULL is not absence but the outline of what never arrived.
sketches.draft:
SELECT self.day, other.presence
FROM calendar AS self
LEFT JOIN encounters AS other
ON self.day = other.day
WHERE other.presence IS NULL;

sketches.mechanism: In Elixir, processes are fully isolated and communicate only by sending messages; a process that crashes simply dies and the message is never received.
sketches.resonance: You can send everything you meant to say, but if the other has already gone, the message sits in a void the runtime quietly discards.
sketches.draft:
pid = spawn(fn -> :ok end)

:timer.sleep(1)

send(pid, :stay)
send(pid, :please)
send(pid, :i_was_not_ready)
